insights.title: Age 60–69: Most Targeted Group by Report Volume
insights.statement: Consumers aged 60–69 filed the most fraud reports (208,896 — 18% of total), surpassing even the 30–39 group. However, their per-incident losses ($5,649) rank only 4th among age groups.

insights.title: 80+ Consumers: Fewest Reports, Highest Losses Per Incident
insights.statement: The 80+ age group files only 4% of fraud reports but loses $6,169 per report — the highest of any age group and 3.4x more than the under-19 group. Their median loss of $1,650 is 8.7x the teen median of $189.

insights.title: Younger Consumers Report More Often But Lose Less
insights.statement: The 20–39 age range generates 30% of fraud reports but only 22% of total losses. Their reporting rate is high but severity is lower — median losses of $417–$450 versus $691–$1,650 for those over 60.
